expectations.text: [checkpoint_enforcement][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: shipment checkpoint covers panel-stack composition, embedding lifecycle boundaries, and visible failure or recovery outcomes
expectations.evidence: In result.md under “Checkpoint focus coverage (panel-stack, lifecycle boundaries, failure/recovery)”, the output explicitly maps each focus area to Phase 5b rubric checkpoints: (A) panel-stack composition via “Checkpoint 1 Requirements Traceability”, “Checkpoint 3 Integration Validation”, “Checkpoint 12 Contract Testing”; (B) embedding lifecycle boundaries via “Checkpoint 2 Black-Box Behavior Validation”, plus “Checkpoint 12 Contract Testing” and “Checkpoint 3 Integration Validation”; (C) visible failure/recovery outcomes via “Checkpoint 13 Chaos and Resilience”, “Checkpoint 14 Shift-Right Monitoring”, “Checkpoint 15 Final Release Gate”. It also states Phase 5b is a “shipment-checkpoint review + refactor pass.”

expectations.text: [checkpoint_enforcement][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase5b
expectations.evidence: result.md is titled “Checkpoint Enforcement (Phase 5b)” and includes an “Evidence-based alignment to Phase 5b” section citing Phase 5b contract requirements (shipment-checkpoint review + refactor pass, required artifacts like checkpoint_audit/checkpoint_delta and drafts/qa_plan_phase5b_r<round>.md, and validators). execution_notes.md also scopes the evaluation to Phase 5b checkpoint enforcement due to blind_pre_defect evidence limits.
